corpora.name: JetStream 3
corpora.intended_use: Exploratory shell-only per-case evidence from an explicitly named JavaScript subset, never an official JetStream score.

requirements: Classify every selected case as JavaScript, WebAssembly, browser, or unsupported.
Prove that cli.js selection does not silently skip required harness capabilities.

blockers: The suite mixes JavaScript, WebAssembly, and multiple workload classes.
No neutral shell subset, phase boundary, or complete expected-case inventory has been audited.
corpora.reason: A future subset may be useful, but it must not be represented as an official JetStream 3 score.

corpora.name: Kraken 1.1
corpora.intended_use: Historical per-case shell evidence using a repository-owned neutral referee.

requirements: Inventory shell globals and browser shims used by every selected case.
Exclude cases whose semantics cannot be supplied identically to every engine.

blockers: Per-file licensing and NOTICE obligations are not inventoried.
The neutral timing and phase-boundary port is not audited.
corpora.reason: The historical workload is eligible for review only as named per-case evidence, not as a pooled modern-engine headline.

corpora.name: Octane
corpora.intended_use: Historical context only; it is not an admission candidate or runner input.

requirements: No capability audit is scheduled while the retired suite remains excluded.

blockers: The suite was retired by its publisher because optimizing for it no longer represented real-world JavaScript performance.
corpora.reason: Publisher retirement makes Octane unsuitable for long-term benchmark governance.

corpora.name: QuickJS-NG V8 Benchmark Suite v7 Port
corpora.intended_use: Named per-case throughput evidence from bench-v8 under an independent repository-owned referee.

requirements: Inventory load, print, timing, and other QuickJS shell dependencies per case.
Prove that one neutral adapter supplies identical harness semantics to all engines.

blockers: Per-workload licensing and NOTICE obligations are not inventoried.
The upstream runner, including its Node benchmark.js path, is opponent-owned and cannot serve as the neutral referee.
corpora.reason: The corpus may be useful after it is separated from the QuickJS-NG runner and independently audited.

corpora.name: QuickJS-NG Web Tooling Benchmark
corpora.intended_use: Whole-program web-tooling shell evidence after neutral capability and phase-boundary audits.

requirements: Provide equivalent stack policy without relying on qjs --stack-size.
Provide equivalent script-mode semantics without relying on qjs --script.

blockers: The documented invocation requires qjs --stack-size 2048 --script dist/cli.js, which qjs-rust does not expose.
Per-file licensing, expected cases, and neutral timing boundaries are not audited.
corpora.reason: Running the upstream invocation today would compare shell capabilities and configuration, not only engine performance.

corpora.name: Speedometer
corpora.intended_use: Browser end-to-end context only; it is not an admission candidate or shell runner input.

requirements: A browser DOM, rendering pipeline, event loop, and web application harness are outside the qjs shell comparison boundary.

blockers: Speedometer measures browser end-to-end web application responsiveness, including DOM and asynchronous phases.
corpora.reason: Its system boundary does not match a pure JavaScript shell engine comparison.

corpora.name: SunSpider 1.0
corpora.intended_use: Preferred first external v2 audit candidate for historical per-case shell evidence under a neutral referee.

requirements: Inventory shell globals and case setup required by PerformanceTests/SunSpider.
Run every selected case through one repository-owned adapter.

blockers: Per-file license inventory and NOTICE disposition are not complete.
Expected cases and the neutral phase boundary are not frozen.
corpora.reason: SunSpider has the clearest small shell-oriented boundary and is the preferred next v2 review after license and NOTICE closure.
